text:
There once was a young dog named Max.

Max loved playing fetch with his human. After countless fetch games, Max grew tired of catching things in the air.

One day, Max discovered a new toy—a wiggly, rubber ball adorned with bells. He bounded over to the yard again for a new adventure.

This time, Max rolled the ball under the big oak tree. He ran back to his human, who surprised him with a high-five.

Max wagged his tail, delighted. “Same time next week?” he asked hopefully.

His human laughed. “It’s more like an every-minute deal.”

The balls rolled every moment, every kick, every fetch. And Max’s ball adventures became the highlight of his days.

One sunny afternoon, while Max was rolling the ball across the grass, he spotted a curious squirrel.

The squirrel stopped and gazed at the bouncing object with curious eyes. Max froze, then wagged his tail with glee, attracting the squirrel’s attention.
